briefdescription.para: Macro to write to video clock generator register.
Macro to read from video clock generator register.

detaileddescription.para: This function looks up and initializes the Video Timing Controller (VTC), Test Pattern Generator (TPG), and Demosaic IP cores.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if all drivers initialized successfully.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if any device is not found.

briefdescription.para: Configures video IP cores for the specified video mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the Test Pattern Generator (TPG), Demosaic core, and Video Timing Controller (VTC) for the specified video mode and timing.

parameterdescription.para: Video mode enumeration specifying resolution and frame rate.

briefdescription.para: Configures the video clock generator for the specified video mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function programs the clock wizard to generate the appropriate pixel clock frequency for the specified video mode based on the TPG pixels per clock configuration. Supports 1080p60, 4K30, and 4K60 video modes.

parameterdescription.para: Video mode enumeration specifying resolution and frame rate.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if clock configuration is successful and locked.
XST_FAILURE if video mode is not supported or clock fails to lock.
Only supports XVIDC_VM_1080_60_P, XVIDC_VM_UHD_30_P, and XVIDC_VM_UHD_60_P.

briefdescription.para: Resets the HLS IP cores in the video pipeline.

detaileddescription.para: This function asserts and then releases the reset signal to all HLS IP cores through the GPIO reset controller.

simplesect.para: Includes appropriate delays for reset assertion and de-assertion.

briefdescription.para: Main function to test the Demosaic video pipeline.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the video pipeline, then tests multiple video modes (1080p60, 4K30, and optionally 4K60) by configuring the clock generator, IP cores, and verifying video lock status.

simplesect.para: 4K60 test only runs if TPG is configured for 2, 4, or 8 pixels per clock.
